sources: Avoma call 2026-05-29 (Zycus Demo Alignment), Jai Rastogi quote
metrics.narrative: Pain is quantified in dollar and time terms even though a formal ROI model hasn't been built. On the 3 Jun 2026 call, Zycus flagged potential dairy consolidation savings of $126k-300k, and Jai Rastogi (CPO) described a cheese spend report that 'would take me months to build' and technology-spend contract mapping that has already taken nearly 8 weeks with no clean answer. These are buyer-stated, not yet formally validated against POC output.

sources: Avoma call 2026-05-29, action items and quotes
champion.narrative: Jai Rastogi (Group Chief Procurement & Supply Chain Officer) is a strong, active champion: he initiated the relationship at a conference, is personally coaching his internal stakeholders ahead of each session, is arranging cross-functional attendance (finance, legal, technology procurement, and now the European team), and offered to broker a reference introduction to a Zycus customer executive for himself and Domino's COO. Per the Next Step log (22 Apr 2026), Jai is ex-Thai Union and has a prior relationship with the deal owner's manager, which appears to give Zycus an inside track ('you guys are fortunate... you've got a front run before we do anything formal').

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-03, competitor notes: prior Coupa/Ariba/iValua deployments
competition.narrative: No active competitive bake-off is underway. Jai has prior hands-on experience with Coupa, SAP Ariba, and iValua from a previous role (Thai Union), and explicitly stated on 29 May 2026 that 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone' for this initiative — meaning Zycus currently holds an inside track rather than facing a live rival. This should be watched, not assumed permanent.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is deep and specific, not generic. Jai described Domino's procurement maturity as 'bodies in spreadsheets,' no common taxonomy across a dozen-plus disparate ERPs, no CLM system with 'significant contract leakage,' and indirect spend that is 'completely unmanaged' (travel contracts handled informally by EAs with 'no rigour'). This was reconfirmed and deepened on the 3 Jun 2026 call with concrete workflow detail (cheese-spend analysis via 10 spreadsheets, 3-week delays; paralegals spending 80% of time on manual contract data entry).

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been discussed in any read source. The deal is pre-POC; commercial terms, MSA/SOW, and signature authority have not yet come up in any call, task, or Next Step entry.

economic_buyer.narrative: Lynn Carruthers (CFO) carries the Executive Sponsor contact role, and Jai stated on 3 Jun 2026 that 'Head of Finance will review POC outcomes for integration decision' and will join the POC discussion for budget approval. However, no Avoma call or task in the read window shows Lynn Carruthers actually present or engaged directly — she is identified on paper but passive to date.

sources: Avoma call 2026-07-03 (RE: DPE x Zycus - Zycus Platform Overview / POC Focus)
decision_process.narrative: The process is reasonably well understood even without a written policy: Jai (CPO) sponsors the evaluation and is coaching internal stakeholders, the Head of Finance reviews POC outcomes for budget approval, and Domino's Chief Legal Officer will separately assess the CLM/contract module once the POC proves value. Multiple regional stakeholders (Michael Gengenbach, Dani de Jong, Maria Oehler in Europe) were looped in on 3 Jul 2026, broadening the committee.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are forming around the POC itself: Jai wants the POC to prove capability on Domino's own data across indirect and technology spend before any internal signoff, and explicitly wants to see 'quick ROI at module level to justify full-suite procurement solution.' No formal RFP or scored criteria exist yet.

engagement.top_event: Provide POC data-extract templates and requirements (spend template for iAnalyze, ANA template, screenshots, historical ANA conversation trails) to Domino's team

items.name: POC scope & platform overview call
items.summary: Zycus (Luke Dougherty, Shirley Islam) walked Domino's (Jai Rastogi, Michael Gengenbach, Maria Oehler, Antje Radüchel-Harders, Dani de Jong) through the platform, agreeing the POC will start with iAnalyze spend analysis before moving to Anna for tactical low-dollar negotiations; three months of indirect spend data was requested as the initial input, with Zycus handling normalization pending Domino's validation.

items.name: Shirley's post-meeting recap and materials
items.summary: Shirley Islam sent Domino's the POC scope materials and the three templates reviewed on the call (iAnalyze first, Anna to follow), asking for an initial data cut on chosen indirect categories to begin iterative normalization; she also flagged the Anna negotiation export will follow on Monday, leaving Domino's to respond with data and category scope.

items.name: Scheduling thread that set up the call
items.summary: Included in the thread is the prior back-and-forth where Michael Gengenbach (new European lead, unable to attend the original demo) proposed Friday 8.30-9.30 CET as an alternative since he's on holiday from the following week, which Zycus accommodated — explaining why the meeting landed on this date.

items.name: Duplicate meeting record with same scheduling email
items.summary: A second logged instance of the same scheduling thread/event description, reflecting calendar/system logging of the rescheduled call rather than new substantive activity.

day_summary.overall: The rescheduled platform overview finally happened, with Zycus walking Domino's European team (Jai Rastogi, Michael Gengenbach, Maria Oehler and others) through the POC scope, confirming it will start with iAnalyze spend insights before layering in Anna for tactical negotiations. Zycus followed up same-day with the promised templates and materials, and the ball is now in Domino's court to check what indirect spend data (Navision/ERP extracts) they can actually pull and confirm scope, likely narrowed to a category like packaging.

cro_panel.intro: A genuinely engaged, champion-led deal with strong, well-articulated pain and a real proof-of-concept in motion, but structurally under-qualified for its stage; the economic buyer has never engaged, no commercial conversation exists, and the deal has sat in Formal Evaluation for 78 days without the qualifying POC actually starting

forecast_read.math: Days to close: 50 (today 9 Jul 2026 vs close 28 Aug 2026). Opportunity entered Formal Evaluation on 22 Apr 2026 (Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c) — 78 days ago, well beyond the ~21-day typical cadence for this stage. No POC kickoff date has been set; the buyer's own 6 Jul 2026 note flags 'concerns about executing POC with disparate DPE resources.' At the current weekly-cadence pace, reaching signature by 28 Aug is unlikely unless data delivery, POC execution, CFO review and contracting all compress into ~7 weeks.
forecast_read.reason: Forecast category (Pipeline) is appropriately conservative, but the 28 Aug 2026 close date is not credible: the POC (the gating milestone for Formal Evaluation) has not started, the Economic Buyer (Lynn Carruthers, CFO) has never engaged, and data extraction across 12-13 disparate Domino's ERPs is still unresolved as of 6 Jul 2026 (~3 days ago).

items.detail: POC depends on Domino's delivering clean, usable data extracts from 12-13 disparate ERP systems (Navision, D365, local ERPs) with no common taxonomy, messy supplier names, and limited category mapping. Jai flagged 6 Jul 'some concerns about executing POC with disparate DPE resources'. 3 Jul call confirmed data extraction is uncertain and Domino's must check Navision/ERP formats and advise feasibility. If data pull stalls or is too messy for Zycus to normalize, POC cannot run and momentum dies.

CFO (Lynn Carruthers, Economic Buyer) has not directly engaged and is passive. Jai Rastogi (CPO) is coaching finance stakeholders to build the internal ROI case via the POC, but CFO approval is not locked. If POC results are thin or the ROI narrative is weak, CFO may defer investment given cost-pressure context (share price fell, transformation funding is tight). Jai stated 'if we see some real tangible results using our data... there'll be a lot of momentum' — the inverse is also true: no tangible results = no momentum.

Wayne McMahon (CIO, Influencer contact role) is mapped but not engaged. POC involves ERP data pulls, integration scoping, and SaaS onboarding; CIO alignment is critical for InfoSec review, data governance, and IT approval during contracting. If CIO surfaces late with objections (security, integration complexity, competing IT priorities), contracting could stall post-POC.

ANA (autonomous negotiation agent) French-language support unconfirmed. Maria Oehler (Domino's Germany) asked 3 Jul whether ANA can communicate with French vendors in French; Zycus answer pending. If ANA is English-only, POC scope may need to narrow to English-speaking markets (Australia, some Europe) or exclude certain suppliers, which could reduce demonstrated savings and weaken the ROI case.

POC execution timeline is compressed: 52 days to close (28 Aug), and POC has not yet launched (awaiting Domino's data extracts, overdue per 6 Jul). If data delivery slips 2-3 weeks, POC runs in late Jul, results land early Aug, leaving minimal time for CFO approval, contracting, legal/InfoSec review, and signature by 28 Aug. Close date may slip to Q3/Q4 2026 if POC or contracting takes longer than planned.

critical_signals.text: No rival is currently in the picture: the champion has run Coupa, SAP Ariba and iValua before but has told Zycus directly he hasn't taken this initiative to market, giving an inside track rather than a bake-off.

The 3 Jul 2026 session (~6 days ago) expanded the POC conversation into Domino's European team for the first time, narrowing the technical scope to spend analytics plus the autonomous negotiation agent, but left the actual data hand-off unresolved.

A Domino's Europe stakeholder asked whether the negotiation agent can operate in French for European suppliers on 3 Jul 2026; the answer is still pending, and it could narrow the POC's usable markets if unresolved.

The buying committee has grown from a single Australian sponsor to a multi-country group spanning Australia, the Netherlands and Germany, which broadens support but also multiplies the number of disparate ERP systems the POC now has to reconcile.

No pricing, budget, or contracting conversation has happened anywhere in the record yet, and the economic buyer who would approve spend has never joined a session, leaving a $450k deal with no commercial thread 50 days from its close date.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is real and recent (buyer email and a five-attendee session within the last 7 days), but the deal's qualification depth is uneven: strong on pain and champion, weak on economic-buyer engagement and commercial process.

north_star_verdict.math: Today is 8 Jul 2026; close date is 28 Aug 2026 = 51 days out. Opp created 22 Apr 2026 (~11 weeks in Formal Evaluation). Sequence to date: demo request (5 May) -> demo scheduled (26 May) -> alignment call (29 May) -> full platform demo (3 Jun) -> POC discussion (9 Jun, not recorded) -> POC planning continuing (22 Jun) -> Europe POC-scoping call (3 Jul) -> Next Step update flagging DPE resourcing concerns (6 Jul). The POC (data extraction, normalization, execution) plus CFO/EB review plus an 'in-principle' award plus contracting all still lie ahead of a 51-day runway; realistically this points to a Q4 2026 close, not 28 Aug.
evidence: Next_Step__c 06 Jul 2026: 'Completed POC planning meeting... concerns about executing POC with disparate DPE resources'
Avoma call 3 Jul 2026: POC scope defined as iAnalyze + ANA across multiple markets, data extracts not yet delivered
Avoma call 29 May 2026: Jai Rastogi: 'Plan for an in principal agreement to award post POC'
north_star_verdict.headline: Sole-source, champion-led evaluation is genuinely live (buyer email 2 Jul 2026, Europe POC-scoping call 3 Jul 2026), but the POC itself has not launched and 51 days to the 28 Aug 2026 close cannot absorb data normalization across 12-13 ERPs, CFO review, and contracting.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (unchanged category; recommend moving Close Date out to Q4 2026 to reflect real POC/contracting lead time)

flags: Convert Lynn Carruthers (CFO, Economic Buyer) from a listed contact role into active engagement before the POC nears completion — Jai's own process depends on 'the Head of Finance' reviewing outcomes for budget approval, and she has not joined a single session to date.
CIO (Wayne McMahon) not engaged; InfoSec/IT approval process unknown. CIO alignment critical for SaaS onboarding and ERP integration during contracting.
POC data extraction overdue and uncertain (6 Jul 'some concerns', 3 Jul 'we've got to check with our Navision team'). If data delivery slips 2-3 weeks, POC timeline compresses and close date at risk.
No quantified savings target or ROI threshold locked; POC results must hit an unstated bar to unlock CFO approval.
No competitor intel; sole-source eval assumed but not confirmed. If POC results are weak, CFO may request multi-vendor comparison.
No competitive RFP is running (Jai: 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone', 29 May 2026) - lock a directional in-principle commitment while sole-sourced, before any market process opens.

Lynn Carruthers (CFO, Executive Sponsor) and Wayne McMahon (CIO, Influencer) remain unengaged on every read call; get one direct touchpoint with Lynn Carruthers before POC results land so budget approval isn't a cold ask.

POC scope has both expanded (AU to Germany/Netherlands) and needs narrowing per the 3 Jul 2026 recommendation to a single starter category (e.g. packaging) - locking this now will speed a demonstrable win ahead of the 28 Aug close date.

Bring Lynn Carruthers into POC progress reviews now, not after results land: Jai is building the internal case alone, and a cost-pressured CFO (Domino's share price fell $161 to $16.68) will need direct visibility to release budget.

Protect the sole-source position by moving fast: no formal RFP is running and Jai has explicitly ruled out shopping the market (29 May 2026); a slow POC creates room for internal governance to force a competitive process.
Domino's spend data spans 12-13 disparate ERPs with no common taxonomy; offering hands-on Zycus data-normalization help now is the single highest-leverage move to get the POC (and the win) unstuck.

The CFO holds the budget but has never engaged directly; getting her into an early POC results review protects against SF's own flagged risk that internal politics could reopen the field to Coupa or iValua.

Jai Rastogi is a strong, multi-threading champion who brought in Domino's Europe unprompted; keep him tightly coached with a realistic POC timeline and a sharp ROI story to sustain the momentum he is building.

Sole-source status is fragile until the POC lands tangible results: Jai warned 'if we see some real tangible results... there'll be a lot of momentum' (29 May 2026) — prioritize a fast, narrow POC (packaging category, per the 3 Jul 2026 Europe suggestion) over a broad multi-market pull to protect that momentum.

Data-normalization risk across 12-13 disparate market ERPs threatens the POC's credibility; scope the first pass to Australia's cleaner data plus one Europe market to guarantee a crisp proof point before expanding further.

Lock a firm data-delivery date with the Domino's Europe team (Michael Gengenbach, Dani de Jong) given 12-13 disparate ERPs — without one, the 28 Aug 2026 close date has no realistic path, since the POC itself hasn't started.
best_practice_check.summary: Two live levers stand out: converting the CFO from a listed name into an engaged sponsor before the POC lands, and closing out a six-week-old reference-customer promise that the champion has repeatedly flagged as important.

customer_preference.evidence: Jai Rastogi told Zycus directly on 29 May 2026 that Domino's has not run a formal market process and Zycus has 'a front run' before anything formal begins — a buyer-voiced preference signal, though not yet a confirmed selection.

competitive_position.summary: Sole-source evaluation: Jai Rastogi stated on 29 May 2026, 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone,' confirming no formal competitive RFP is running. Coupa, SAP Ariba, and iValua are referenced only as tools Jai personally implemented in prior roles (including at Thai Union) — background comparison points that lend him credibility, not active bids against Zycus here. The single strongest 'threat' to this deal is not a rival vendor but the POC itself failing to produce a tangible enough proof point to convert Jai's in-principle intent into a signed award.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely engaged, champion-led deal with strong, well-articulated pain and a real proof-of-concept in motion, but structurally under-qualified for its stage: the economic buyer has never engaged, no commercial conversation exists, and the deal has sat in Formal Evaluation for 78 days without the qualifying POC actually starting.

deal_risk.text: Data-extraction risk — Domino's runs 12-13 disparate ERPs (Navision, D365, local systems) with no common taxonomy and inconsistent supplier naming; a Domino's Europe attendee confirmed on 3 Jul 2026 that indirect-spend extraction 'will be more challenging.'

Budget-pressure backdrop — Jai referenced Domino's share price falling from $161 to $16.68 and confirmed transformation funding must be self-funded from procurement savings, raising the bar the POC's business case must clear.
Timeline compression — 50 days remain to the 28 Aug 2026 close date, and the POC (data delivery, execution, CFO review) has not yet started.

win_position.text: Champion is genuinely driving the process — Jai Rastogi has personally coached stakeholders ahead of three sessions, expanded the committee to Europe, and offered to broker an executive reference introduction (29 May 2026, still open).
No active competitor — Jai stated on 29 May 2026 he 'hasn't gone out to market to anyone,' despite direct prior experience with Coupa, SAP Ariba and iValua from a previous role.
Economic buyer never engaged — Lynn Carruthers (CFO, Executive Sponsor role) has not appeared on any read call despite Jai naming her as the budget approver on 3 Jun 2026.
78 days in Formal Evaluation with no POC launched yet — data delivery, the gating dependency, was still unresolved as of 6 Jul 2026 (~3 days ago).

deal_momentum.text: Real two-way buyer engagement in the last 7 days — a Domino's Europe stakeholder replied by email on 2 Jul 2026 and a full POC-scoping session ran on 3 Jul 2026 with five Domino's attendees across three countries.
Deliverables are landing — Zycus sent the promised iAnalyze, ANA, and screenshot POC templates immediately following the 3 Jul 2026 session.
The core buyer-owed deliverable (spend data extracts) remains outstanding since it was first requested around 29 May-11 Jun 2026, and Jai flagged execution concerns as recently as 6 Jul 2026.
Rep has sent two follow-up emails (7-8 Jul 2026) with no buyer reply yet as of 9 Jul 2026 — early, not yet a stall, but worth watching.

customer_commitment.text: Jai's own words on 29 May 2026: 'if we see some real tangible results using our data... there'll be a lot of momentum behind it' — he has committed internal stakeholders and cross-functional time (finance, legal, technology, and now Europe) to the process.
Commitment is real but concentrated in the champion; the CFO and CIO (Wayne McMahon), both named contact roles, have not personally shown up to validate that commitment.
